name: scrapeless-scraping-browser
description: Drive the Scrapeless cloud browser to open pages, discover DOM, extract structured data, fill forms, screenshot, and route traffic through residential proxies with anti-detection fingerprinting. Use when the user asks to scrape, extract, pull, grab, or fetch data from a page; fill out or submit a form; log in or sign in to a site; take a screenshot; download a file; automate a browser task; or run requests through a specific geography or a residential proxy.
whenNotToUse: For plain HTML fetches of static pages, WebFetch or curl is cheaper. For authenticated multi-step flows that outlive the session TTL, use the @scrapeless-ai SDK / exported ScrapelessAPI directly. For MCP-first setups, use scrapeless-mcp-server.
author: Scrapeless AI

environment.notes: Fallback: config file at ~/.scrapeless/config.json (user-only 0600 permissions). Config takes priority over env; only SCRAPELESS_API_KEY is read from the environment. The scrapeless-mcp-server reads SCRAPELESS_KEY (different name) — not used by this skill.

examples.prompt: Open Hacker News, extract the top five stories with title, URL, author, score, and comment count, and return as JSON.

sequence.note: Discovery. Agent reads the returned DOM to identify the real row/title/score selectors — does NOT guess them from memory.
Extraction. Selectors here come from what the agent just read in the get html output, not from training data.

examples.prompt: Fill the job application at <URL> with my resume at ~/docs/resume.txt, screenshot the form, and stop before the final submit so I can review.

sequence.note: Discovery for interaction. Returns @e1, @e2, … refs with roles and labels — agent maps prompt fields to refs from this output.
All refs in this block come from the snapshot -i output above.
Stop here. Do NOT click the submit ref until the human approves review.png.

examples.prompt: Open Booking.com as if I were in Tokyo — Japanese residential proxy, iOS Safari, Tokyo timezone — and pull the top five hotels for June 15–18.

sequence.cmd: new-session --proxy-country JP --proxy-city tokyo --platform iOS --timezone Asia/Tokyo --languages ja,en
sequence.note: Session identity set BEFORE open — proxy and fingerprint can't change mid-session. Residential-proxy allocation (especially non-US countries) can take 30–60s and occasionally 503s on first attempt; wrap new-session in a 3–5 try retry loop.
Discovery. Agent does not assume [data-testid='property-card'] is still the selector — Booking A/B tests aggressively. Reads real DOM first.
Selectors here are the ones the agent just saw in get html.

examples.prompt: Rerun this with session recording enabled — the extraction came back empty yesterday and I want to see why.

sequence.note: Recording enabled at session creation — can't be toggled mid-session.
Returns a WSS URL. Agent gives it to the human so they can watch the session in real time.
Inspect state to compare with the (empty) result from yesterday's run.
